intermittently fail when run alongside other tests. Changed to a
smoke test that verifies no exception is thrown.

- Phase 2 (post-setup): Traefik, Rustfs, PgAdmin, Smallstep

commit.message:
fix(ci): group SDK deps in dependabot and ignore sha2 major bumps

Dependabot was creating separate PRs for convergio-types and
convergio-db, but both come from the same git repo tag. When only
one is bumped, the Cargo.lock becomes inconsistent and CI fails
with --locked. Grouping them ensures they're bumped together.

Also ignore sha2 major bumps (0.10→0.11) to stay aligned with SDK.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replenish RADIO queues via Yandex Music API instead of EOV

RADIO/wave queues only have ~2 tracks. sync_state_from_eov does NOT
replenish them — that's the YM app's job via REST API. Now the active
device fetches new tracks from get_rotor_station_tracks() and pushes
the expanded playable_list to Ynison before advancing the index.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: harden cache-memory pipeline against exec-bit persistence and instruction injection (#1917)

* Initial plan

* fix: harden cache-memory against exec-bit and injection attacks

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/github/gh-aw-firewall/sessions/b0ee2cdb-eb78-4742-995b-6ccf4573fd7e

* fix: tighten injection scan, add quarantine and idempotent update

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/github/gh-aw-firewall/sessions/b0ee2cdb-eb78-4742-995b-6ccf4573fd7e

* fix: address review feedback on cache-memory hardening

- Guard find with directory existence check to avoid failures when
  cache dir is missing (e.g., when prior setup step failed)
- Preserve directory structure in quarantine instead of flattening
  paths, avoiding filename collisions and evidence loss
- Escape head output with sed to prevent GitHub Actions workflow
  command injection from malicious file content
- Apply cache key and restore-keys TTL transformations independently
  so partially updated workflows are correctly repaired

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Increase terrain cache margins and pan thresholds for smoother low-zoom pan

Doubled terrain margin tiles (20->40, 14->28, 8->16) and pan thresholds
(80->200, 50->140, 25->80) at low zoom. Faster pan lerp was hitting old
thresholds too often, causing frequent terrain redraws during panning.

commit.message:
Fix startup rendering and UI anchoring

Defer the initial scene transition to the first rendered frame so menu construction occurs after Ursina UI initialization. Explicitly parent menu text and button widgets to camera.ui and simplify background layering to avoid blank startup screens.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Worker deploy crash by stubbing unused react-player backends (#22)

`react-player` bundles all player backends via `React.lazy` dynamic
imports, but Vite inlines them into a single SSR chunk evaluated at
Worker startup. Two backends crash at module-evaluation time in the
Workers runtime:

- `@vimeo/player` — `throw new Error("Sorry, the Vimeo Player API is not
available in this browser.")`
- `imsc` (via `dashjs`) —
`window.getComputedStyle(document.documentElement)` at top level

Since this app only uses YouTube, alias all 8 non-YouTube backends to a
no-op component via Vite `resolve.alias`:

```ts
// vite.config.mts
resolve: {
  alias: [
    "dash-video-element/react",
    "hls-video-element/react",
    "vimeo-video-element/react",
    // ... 5 more
  ].map((pkg) => ({
    find: new RegExp(`^${pkg.replace("/", "\\/")}$`),
    replacement: noopPlayerStub,
  })),
},
```

- **`src/stubs/noop-player.ts`** — `export default function NoopPlayer()
{ return null; }`
- **`vite.config.mts`** — resolve aliases for all non-YouTube player
backends

Worker bundle: 3,069 KB → 942 KB. SSR bundle: 3,506 KB → 530 KB.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: force-push diagram branch and handle existing PR on retry (#53)

On workflow re-runs the temp branch already exists; --force push
overwrites it. gh pr create --or true skips the error if the PR
already exists; gh pr merge targets the branch explicitly.

commit.message:
Split scene cache: overlay-only (no terrain) for near-instant builds

The ~1s lag when zoom settled was caused by re-rendering 60,000+ terrain
tiles into the scene cache. Now the scene cache stores ONLY overlay layers
(territories, roads, sea routes, towns, caravans) as a transparent canvas.

Per-frame render at low zoom:
1. Terrain — uses its own existing margin-based cache (already fast)
2. Overlay cache blit — single drawImage (instant)
3. Player marker + hover — drawn per-frame (cheap)

Cache build is now ~10x faster since it skips all terrain tile rendering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Dead Code & Asset Audit (#8368)

* Agent: Dead Code & Asset Audit

* Remove confirmed dead code: collectPatchDurations, isNumericCallExpression, isValidIdentifierName, hasBlankLineAfterOpeningBrace

These four exported functions have zero references across the entire codebase
(no imports, no test coverage, not re-exported through any public API index).
Each was verified by exhaustive grep across all src/ and test/ .ts files.

- collectPatchDurations (runtime-wrapper/patch-utils.ts): filters patch
  history for apply durations; never called, never re-exported from the
  runtime-wrapper index.
- isNumericCallExpression (format/printer/type-guards.ts): checked for
  sqr/sqrt call nodes; never imported by any printer or test file.
- isValidIdentifierName (format/printer/type-guards.ts): regex identifier
  check; never imported anywhere in the format workspace.
- hasBlankLineAfterOpeningBrace (format/printer/source-text.ts): blank-line
  detection helper; never called from any printer path.

Removal of PatchHistoryEntry from the patch-utils import is a cascading
cleanup of the now-unused type import.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SimulatorLife/GMLoop/sessions/92cdd875-839e-40ea-b69f-8980b0d95cf5

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: jonathancollas <10053522+jonathancollas@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor InfoSnapshot to bitmask wire format

Replace per-field MarketData + SecurityStatus messages with a single
InfoSnapshot message using a u32 bitmask for 22 fields. Each set bit
corresponds to a field present as an i64 value in the payload.

- WireProtocol: add WriteInfoSnapshot with field bitmask constants
- SubscriptionManager: replace SendInfoSnapshot (17+ per-field sends)
  and SendInfoFields with single WriteInfoSnapshot calls
- Remove old WriteMarketData/WriteSecurityStatus methods
- Max message size: 192 bytes (header + securityId + mask + 22×i64)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix timestring word-wrap to prevent splitting on spaces within time text

Prevents line breaks inside timestring (e.g., 'four minutes past one')
which was causing partial timestring overflow and duplication with mixed
formatting (bold + regular text).

Now spaces inside the timestring are skipped as break points, ensuring
the entire timestring stays on one line and renders fully in bold.

commit.message:
feat(logger): support string arguments in static log methods

Static methods like Logger.info() now accept either:
  Logger.info("simple message")
  Logger.info({ namespace: "x" }, "message with options")

Previously only the object form worked. The string form is more
ergonomic for quick logging and matches README examples.

Uses TypeScript overloads for type safety and runtime argument
normalization for the actual dispatch.

commit.message:
Fix(blueprint): Force slot_code to string

Jinja2 auto-coerces numeric-looking values to integers,
which drops leading zeros (e.g. 0142 becomes 142). The
Guesty API rejects integers and requires a string value.

Add the | string filter at the slot_code variable
assignment and in the service call value template to
preserve the original text representation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
Initial implementation: PowerGrid ZeroOps SRE Agent Lab

- 5 microservices: portal-web (React), outage-api (Python), meter-api (.NET),
  grid-status-api (Node.js), notification-svc (Go)
- Bicep IaC with ACA/AKS dual deployment, observability, SRE Agent, Arc VM
- 8 break/fix scenarios across 4 categories (service outage, OOM, perf
  regression, container crash, bad image, bad config, disk, logs)
- CLI simulator (rich-based, Zava-style) with live dashboards
- SRE Agent config: subagents, MCP connectors (ServiceNow, Datadog,
  Dynatrace), response plans, scheduled tasks
- Knowledge base: architecture doc + 6 runbooks with KQL queries
- K8s manifests for AKS deployment option
- azd-compatible setup with one-command deployment
- Managed Identity / Entra ID throughout (zero secrets)
- Public GitHub ready: README, LICENSE (MIT), CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY,
  DevContainer, CI workflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tooling): Protocol System Enhancements Phase 4 — grammar, hover, and completion (#600)

* Initial plan

* feat(tooling): update grammar and analysis for protocol generics, associated types, impl type params, and where clauses

- tree-sitter: add TypeParamList, TypeParam, AssociatedTypeDeclaration,
  AssociatedTypeBinding, WhereClause, WherePredicate rules; update
  ProtocolDeclaration and ImplBlock to support new syntax
- lezer: update grammar to match — TypeParamList, TypeParam,
  AssociatedTypeDeclaration, AssociatedTypeBinding, WhereClause,
  WherePredicate, updated ProtocolDeclaration and ImplBlock
- rebuild lezer parser from updated grammar
- index.js: add `type` and `where` to keyword completions and style
  tags; add highlight rules for TypeParam, WherePredicate, and
  AssociatedType nodes
- AST visitor: extend walk_stmt to fully traverse ProtocolDeclaration
  (constraints, associated types, method params/return types) and
  ImplBlock (protocol name, target type, where clause, associated
  type bindings)
- query.rs: add declaration-context fallback in resolve_symbol that
  tries find_member_by_suffix when direct lookup fails and no
  field_base — enables hover on protocol method names and struct
  field names in their own declarations
- symbol_index.rs: add collect_protocol_completions() for protocol
  method completion items; add tests for protocol hover and
  completion

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/topaxi/tlang/sessions/02a5ae38-fb4e-4fec-8c80-a877bc6dc6fd

Co-authored-by: topaxi <213788+topaxi@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(analysis): fix detached doc comment on find_member_by_suffix

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/topaxi/tlang/sessions/02a5ae38-fb4e-4fec-8c80-a877bc6dc6fd

Co-authored-by: topaxi <213788+topaxi@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tooling): address review feedback on grammar, visitor traversal, and hover fallback

- tree-sitter: allow lowercase identifiers in type_param and
  where_predicate name fields (choice of identifier | type_identifier);
  use type_annotation for impl_block protocol/type fields to support
  multi-segment paths like foo::Display and foo::MyType
- lezer: use TypeAnnotation for ImplBlock protocol and type fields to
  support paths
- find_node.rs: add is_declaration_name flag to FoundNode; set it via
  with_declaration_name_context() when visiting protocol method names,
  associated type names, struct field names, and associated type
  binding names in impl blocks; also visit type_params on protocols,
  protocol methods, associated types and impl blocks; visit where
  predicate name idents; visit protocol method default bodies with
  proper scope
- query.rs: gate the declaration-context fallback on
  found.is_declaration_name (instead of field_base.is_none()) to
  prevent mis-resolving undefined locals to unrelated members
- visit.rs: add type_params traversal for ProtocolDeclaration,
  ProtocolMethodSignature, AssociatedTypeDeclaration,
  AssociatedTypeBinding, and ImplBlock; add where predicate name
  traversal; do NOT walk protocol method bodies in the generic
  walk_stmt (declaration_analyzer already does so with proper scope)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/topaxi/tlang/sessions/0e018ce2-09d5-4c34-966e-1c11ed82440d

commit.message:
Fix scene cache: smooth zoom transitions, no freeze on fast pan

- During zoom transitions (lerp animation), use lightweight direct render
  instead of scene cache — avoids expensive rebuild every frame
- Scene cache only engages after zoom stable for ~12 frames (~200ms)
- On fast-pan cache miss, fall back to direct render for one frame instead
  of blocking to rebuild (eliminates the freeze-then-load stutter)
- Increased scene cache margin from 60% to 100% (full viewport in each
  direction) so fast panning rarely exceeds cached bounds
- Restored proper terrain pan thresholds for low-zoom direct render path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Wire M3Exporter::exportTextures fileManifest entries in exportAsOBJ

Document that M3Exporter::exportTextures() matches the original JS source,
which also returns an empty map (M3 texture export is not implemented
upstream). Replace the TODO comment in exportAsOBJ with actual fileManifest
wiring that iterates validTextures, matching the JS pattern:
  for (const [texFileDataID, texInfo] of validTextures)
    fileManifest?.push({ type: 'PNG', fileDataID: texFileDataID, file: texInfo.matPath });

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: NathanNatNat <33712044+NathanNatNat@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: wow.export.cpp

commit.message:
fix(logger): preserve custom transport when reconfiguring other options

Logger.configure() used config.transport (incoming partial) to decide
whether to create a default transport. If a user set a custom transport
and later called configure({ level: "debug" }), the custom transport
was discarded because config.transport was undefined.

Now uses Logger.config.transport (merged config) so previously-set
transports are preserved unless explicitly overridden.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
